name: Alpha Futures
description_short: Alpha Futures is a UK-based futures prop firm offering Standard, Advanced, and Zero plans with EOD trailing drawdown, 90% profit splits, and weekly payouts after five winning days.
description_long:
## What Alpha Futures Is and Who Runs It

Alpha Futures is a UK-based futures prop firm founded in 2024 and operated by Alpha Futures Limited out of Harpenden, United Kingdom. It sits in a crowded field of simulated-funding shops, but has clawed its way to a 4.9/5 Trustpilot rating across 2,500+ reviews — one of the highest trader-satisfaction scores in the category. The firm claims $70M in performance fees paid to 175,000+ Qualified Analysts across 140+ countries, making it one of the fastest-growing futures prop firms in the industry despite its recent launch.

The pitch is simple: pass a one-step evaluation, trade a simulated futures account up to $750K, and keep 90% of profits. Where Alpha Futures actually differentiates itself is in the risk structure — specifically an **end-of-day (EOD) trailing drawdown** that locks at your starting balance instead of trailing intraday wicks — and in payout mechanics that feel closer to a real trading firm than a marketing funnel.

## Core Features and What Makes It Unique

Alpha Futures offers three distinct product lines rather than one bloated plan matrix. **Standard** is the traditional two-step evaluation with a 70% split scaling to 90% after five payouts. **Advanced** pays 90% from day one, drops the consistency rule on the qualified phase, and allows unrestricted news trading. **Zero** is instant-funded (no evaluation) at smaller account sizes for traders who want to skip the challenge phase entirely.

The standout mechanic across all three is the EOD trailing drawdown. Most futures prop firms use intraday trailing drawdown, which means a wick against you at 2 a.m. can blow your account even if you close green. Alpha Futures only calculates drawdown at end-of-day close, and once you reach your starting balance in profit, the drawdown locks. That's a materially safer risk model for anyone trading volatile futures like NQ or CL.

Other notable features: **bi-weekly payouts on Standard**, **weekly payouts on Advanced and Zero after five winning days**, a **$15,000 max performance fee per cycle** (among the largest in the industry), and same-day payout processing via ACH, Wire, SWIFT, Wise, and Rise. The 2-minute news buffer applies only to Standard and Zero qualified accounts — Advanced traders can trade news freely.

## Pricing Breakdown

Alpha Futures pricing runs on evaluation fees rather than pure monthly subscriptions, with a **$149 one-time activation fee** on Standard and Advanced plans once you pass the evaluation. Here are the current numbers:

**Standard $50K:** $79/month evaluation, $149 activation. 70% split scaling to 90%.

**Standard $100K:** $159/month evaluation, $149 activation.

**Advanced $50K:** $139/month evaluation, $149 activation. 90% split from day one.

**Advanced $100K:** $279/month evaluation, $149 activation.

**Zero $25K:** $79/month, no activation fee, instant funded, 90% split.

**Zero $50K:** $119/month, no activation fee, instant funded.

**Zero $100K:** $239/month, no activation fee.

Zero Qualified account resets run $399 ($25K) to $499 ($50K) — steep, but the trade-off is skipping evaluation entirely. Larger $250K and $750K Alpha Prime accounts exist for scaled traders but are gated behind performance. Always check the Alpha Futures website for current pricing since prop firm promos rotate frequently.

## Honest Pros — What Alpha Futures Genuinely Does Well

**Trustpilot rating is real.** 4.9/5 across 2,500+ reviews is not something you fake at that volume. Payout complaints — the death knell of any prop firm — are rare in the review corpus.

**EOD trailing drawdown is a serious edge.** Locking the drawdown at starting balance once you clear it means you actually get to keep your runway after your first payout. Most competitors reset the buffer the moment you withdraw. Alpha Futures does not.

**Three distinct plans, not one bloated ladder.** Standard, Advanced, and Zero each solve for a different trader psychology — evaluation-first, fast-scale, or instant-funded. That's cleaner than the ten-tier menus other firms hide behind.

**Payout rails and speed are legitimate.** ACH, Wire, SWIFT, Wise, and Rise cover most of the world, and same-day processing after milestone is unusually fast for the space.

**Advanced plan has no consistency rule** on the qualified phase, which is the single most annoying constraint on other prop firms. If you have one green day that's 60% of your monthly PnL, you don't get punished.

## Honest Cons — Real Limitations

**No algorithmic trading, period.** Expert Advisors, bots, and HFT are prohibited on every Alpha Futures product. If you trade automated strategies, this firm is a hard no.

**Standard plan makes you grind for the 90% split.** You get 70% until you've completed five payouts — that's realistically two to three months of consistent performance before you're on full split.

**News buffer on Standard and Zero qualified.** A 2-minute buffer around scheduled news isn't crippling, but scalpers and news traders will feel it. Only Advanced escapes this.

**Platform locks at purchase.** You pick your platform (Tradovate, NinjaTrader, Rithmic, etc.) when you buy and can't switch mid-account. That's a UX regression compared to firms that let you swap freely.

**UK entity adds friction for some US traders.** Payout method availability and tax reporting can be more of a paperwork exercise than with US-domiciled competitors.

**Zero account resets are pricey.** $399–$499 to reset a Zero Qualified account is a lot if you tilt out early. The instant-funding convenience has a real cost baked in.

## Who Should Use Alpha Futures — And Who Shouldn't

**Use Alpha Futures if:** you trade futures discretionarily (especially day trading NQ, ES, MNQ, CL, GC), you want EOD risk rules instead of intraday trailing, you value payout reliability over gimmick promotions, and you don't run bots. The Advanced plan in particular is one of the better propositions in the market for a serious day trader who wants 90% from day one and can trade news.

**Do NOT use Alpha Futures if:** you run automated strategies (banned across every plan), you trade forex or equities primarily (this is futures-only), you need a copy-trade or MT4/MT5 workflow, or you want a firm with a longer operating track record than 2024. Alpha Futures is credible but young — if a five-year audit history matters to you, look elsewhere.

## How It Compares to Topstep and Apex Trader Funding

**vs. Topstep:** Topstep is the elder statesman of futures prop firms with a decade-plus track record and a more mature education stack. Alpha Futures beats Topstep on profit split (90% vs. 90% but Topstep requires you to keep the first $10K entirely), on drawdown model (EOD lock vs. Topstep's trailing that resets less favorably), and on payout speed. Topstep wins on brand trust, longevity, and its Trading Combine data feedback.

**vs. Apex Trader Funding:** Apex is cheaper on promo pricing (heavy 80–90% discounts most weekends) and offers up to 20 accounts per trader. Alpha Futures has cleaner drawdown mechanics, a better-rated support experience, and a more transparent payout structure. Apex has occasionally had rule-change controversies that Alpha Futures — so far — has not. If price is everything, Apex during a promo is hard to beat. If you want rules that don't shift under you, Alpha Futures is the safer pick.

## The Bottom Line

Alpha Futures is one of the better futures prop firms to launch in 2024, and the Trustpilot score, EOD drawdown mechanics, and payout reliability back that up. It's not the cheapest option and it's actively hostile to algo traders, but for a discretionary futures day trader who wants a firm that treats winning days as a feature rather than a bug, Alpha Futures deserves a serious look. Start with the Advanced $50K or a Zero $25K to test the payout pipeline yourself before scaling up.
